The most common organisms in subacute endocarditis include Streptococcus viridans, Enterococcus, and Staphylococcus aureus.
Given his smoking and valve abnormality, Staph aureus is a possibility, but S. viridans is more common in subacute cases.
However, the presentation here, especially the Osler's nodes and nail hemorrhages, points towards subacute bacterial endocarditis.
So, putting it all together, the most likely diagnosis is subacute bacterial endocarditis, likely caused by Streptococcus viridans.
